codeReview.summary: The confidence-threshold-slider feature is fully implemented according to the specification. The ConfidenceThresholdSlider component provides: slider range 0.1-0.9 (10% to 90%) with default value 0.5 (50%), adjustable slider that immediately filters detections via CoverageOverlay, collapsible/minimizable UI that doesn't obstruct the camera view, and localStorage persistence via insurescope_confidenceThreshold key. Integration is clean: CoverageOverlay already supported confidenceThreshold prop, CameraView integrates the slider and passes threshold to overlay, AppContext manages threshold state with localStorage. The component exports useful constants (MIN_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD, MAX_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD, DEFAULT_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD, THRESHOLD_STEP) and formatThresholdPercentage helper. Comprehensive test coverage with 36 tests (28 component + 8 AppContext threshold tests).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed the documented patterns in AGENTS.md: component files use PascalCase .jsx, tests are co-located, path alias @/ is used for imports, and Tailwind utility classes are used exclusively.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ConfidenceThresholdSlider.jsx follows all conventions: uses @/ path alias, Tailwind classes only, co-located test file with .test.jsx extension

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the frontend-worker skill procedure correctly: invoked skills first, ran baseline tests, implemented using TDD (tests written before implementation), verified with npm test, and performed manual verification with agent-browser.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows skill invocation at start, test file created before implementation, all 392 tests passing, manual browser verification completed

sharedStateObservations.observation: CoverageOverlay component already supported confidenceThreshold prop - the worker correctly identified this existing capability and simply wired it up from CameraView through the AppContext. This demonstrates good code reuse and understanding of existing architecture.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: CoverageOverlay.jsx line 89 shows DEFAULT_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.5 and confidenceThreshold prop with default; CameraView.jsx passes confidenceThreshold from useAppContext()

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker exported constants (MIN_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD, MAX_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD, DEFAULT_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD, THRESHOLD_STEP) and formatThresholdPercentage helper function, making the component reusable and testable. This follows good library design patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ConfidenceThresholdSlider.jsx exports 5 named exports including constants and helper function

summary: The confidence-threshold-slider feature passes review. All expected behavior is implemented: slider with range 0.1-0.9 and default 0.5, collapsible UI positioned to not obstruct camera view, localStorage persistence, and real-time filtering of detections via CoverageOverlay. Test coverage is comprehensive with 36 tests covering filtering at different thresholds, default value, persistence, collapsible behavior, edge cases, and input validation. No blocking issues found. Code quality is high with proper documentation and adherence to project conventions.
